exit_statement: A bounded real FEM workflow can be installed, run, audited, explained, updated, and repeated without relying on hidden state or private knowledge.

gates.title: Numerical Trust
next_1x_focus: deepen retained qualification evidence beyond compact closed-form and patch fixtures
keep broad physics coverage at qualification while labeling any new experimental families outside the release gate
required: core solver families have benchmark-backed baselines
benchmark docs include reference values, tolerances, and automation status
solver limitations are visible in docs and product-facing descriptions
result drift is detectable across releases
acceptable_limitations: some advanced solver families can remain experimental if clearly labeled
blockers: claiming industrial trust from demos or hand-picked screenshots alone

gates.title: Workflow And Asset Durability
next_1x_focus: tighten workflow graph, dataset, report, and snapshot lineage into one durable asset story
make cleanup and replay behavior visible across workbench and headless SDK paths
required: workflow graphs and dataset contracts are stable enough to export, import, replay, and explain
results can be traced back to model inputs, material data, workflow version, operator version, runtime path, and agent context
snapshots, reports, and result bundles have visible lifecycle and cleanup rules
optimization metrics are first-class report contracts, not informal notes
acceptable_limitations: project persistence can still be file-first if the file contract is clear
blockers: important result meaning depends on hidden UI state or untracked local state

gates.title: Headless SDK Credibility
next_1x_focus: keep Rust SDK as the reference implementation for material research workflows
document and narrow Python and Elixir parity gaps without blocking Rust-led proof
required: Rust headless SDK supports real workflow initialization, plan, run, and report paths
material research examples include optimization profiles and candidate-level score explanations
CLI and SDK surfaces produce machine-readable outputs suitable for AI agents and CI
frontend wasm Python automation and backend headless SDK responsibilities are clearly separated
acceptable_limitations: Python and Elixir parity can lag behind Rust for advanced material research if the gap is documented
blockers: headless paths are only demos and cannot run without frontend-only behavior

gates.title: Installer, Update, And Disk Hygiene
next_1x_focus: wire the disk hygiene check into installer apply and rollback dry-runs
surface remote artifact retention and local purge state in the installer UI
required: standard install paths are documented for supported platforms
update source, download, staging, apply, rollback, and cleanup behavior are visible
old residue cleanup is explicit and safe
large build/download artifacts do not silently accumulate on developer or user machines
acceptable_limitations: some platform packagers can remain manual if the support level is clearly marked
blockers: users cannot predict where files go or how to recover from a failed update

gates.title: Agent, Mesh, And Runtime Authority
next_1x_focus: harden one-orchestrator authority and offline mesh mode as product contracts
make operator pull, cache, and cleanup behavior auditable from runtime logs
required: agent authority rules are explicit
one agent cannot be ambiguously controlled by multiple orchestrators
offline direct mesh and orchestrated control-plane modes are separated
operator pull/cache/cleanup behavior is visible and auditable
remote runtime actions are logged with enough context for diagnosis
acceptable_limitations: direct mesh can remain narrower than orchestrated mode if its boundary is honest
blockers: runtime authority is ambiguous or hidden behind UI convenience

gates.title: Security And Audit
next_1x_focus: finish credential, certificate, and remote-control storage boundaries before 2.0 freeze
turn privileged deployment and update actions into visible audit events
required: certificate, token, and remote-control behavior is documented
sensitive configuration is not committed into project data
privileged installer and remote actions are audited
user-visible configuration distinguishes editable and read-only settings
acceptable_limitations: enterprise policy integrations can wait for 2.x if local trust behavior is solid
blockers: secrets, certificates, or remote credentials are handled as ordinary project files

gates.title: Workbench And UX Stability
next_1x_focus: keep workbench flows aligned with SDK/runtime concepts rather than private UI state
protect supported resolutions and UI automation selectors as product contracts
required: core UI flows avoid overlapping or unusable layouts at supported resolutions
workbench and SDK use the same concepts for workflows, materials, results, and reports
UI automation selectors stay product-owned and stable
error recovery paths are visible to users
acceptable_limitations: advanced rendering can remain staged if current visualization limits are clear
blockers: successful workflows require fragile UI-only gestures that cannot be automated or recovered

gates.title: Documentation As Product Infrastructure
next_1x_focus: keep the docs book, navigation matrix, release posture, and troubleshooting paths aligned
document capability limits near the feature that exposes each limit
required: the central docs book is coherent enough for humans and large-model agents
release posture, architecture boundaries, SDK paths, installer behavior, and troubleshooting are findable from the docs map
limitations are documented near the relevant capability
acceptable_limitations: some desktop-facing HTML mirrors can lag briefly if repository docs remain source-of-truth
blockers: critical operational knowledge exists only in chat history or local memory
